name: Yagi-Uda Array
description: A directional antenna array consisting of a driven element (typically a dipole) and multiple parasitic elements including reflectors and directors. The parasitic elements are not directly connected to the transmission line but are electromagnetically coupled to the driven element, creating a highly directional radiation pattern with significant forward gain.

driven_length.name: Driven Element Length
driven_length.description: Length of the driven dipole element, typically close to half wavelength

reflector_length.name: Reflector Length
reflector_length.description: Length of the reflector element, typically 5% longer than driven element

director_length.name: Director Length
director_length.description: Length of director elements, typically 5-10% shorter than driven element

reflector_spacing.name: Reflector Spacing
reflector_spacing.description: Distance between driven element and reflector

director_spacing.name: Director Spacing
director_spacing.description: Distance between driven element and first director, or between directors

num_directors.name: Number of Directors
num_directors.description: Number of director elements in the array

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves optimizing element lengths and spacings to achieve desired gain and impedance matching through electromagnetic coupling between parasitic elements.

steps.title: Determine Operating Frequency
steps.description: Calculate free-space wavelength and establish design frequency

steps.title: Design Driven Element
steps.description: Set driven element length for resonance, accounting for mutual coupling effects

steps.title: Position and Size Reflector
steps.description: Place reflector behind driven element with appropriate length for maximum reflection

steps.title: Design Director Elements
steps.description: Size and position directors for progressive phase shift and beam focusing

steps.title: Optimize Element Spacing
steps.description: Adjust spacings to maximize forward gain while maintaining impedance match

steps.title: Calculate Input Impedance
steps.description: Determine driving point impedance considering mutual coupling between all elements

steps.title: Verify Radiation Pattern
steps.description: Calculate directivity and front-to-back ratio to confirm performance

equations.resonantFrequency: f_r = c / (2 * L_eff) where L_eff accounts for end effects and coupling
equations.inputImpedance: Z_in = V_1 / I_1 = Z_11 + sum(Z_1n * I_n / I_1) for n = 2 to N
equations.gain: G = D * eta where D = 4*pi / (integral of |F(theta,phi)|^2 over 4*pi)
equations.radiationPattern: E(theta,phi) = sum(I_n * exp(-j*k*r_n*cos(theta)) * f_n(theta,phi)) for all elements n

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Method of moments with mutual impedance matrix Z_mn between elements m and n
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Superposition of individual element patterns with appropriate phase relationships
keyAssumptions: Thin wire approximation valid for wire radius << wavelength
Ground plane effects negligible for elevated arrays
Linear current distribution approximation on each element
Lossless conductors assumed for gain calculations

references: Balanis, C.A., 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 10
Stutzman, W.L. and Thiele, G.A., 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Chapter 7
Volakis, J.L., 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 11
